inline_comments.message: what is the benefit of having this as env variable?
please pass this from the parent chart using helm hook (you need to define secret name instead apart from uid)
This env variable will reflect the actual DB used either common or local, it will be evaluated by the common.mariadbService from the deployment of the component.

The whole mariadb-galera part is passed from the parent chart using the hook (&localMariadb), I suppose if any change presented on the parent chart ("mariadb-galera" "db" "externaSecret") it will reflect here. I hardcoded this value just to pass helm lint.
same answer of previous comment, this value presented just to pass helm lint, there is a hook on the parent leaf (mariadb-galera)

I get what this env variable will hold but it's not a sensitive information I think no?
In that can you can evaluate whatever you need to evaluate here directly instead of going through the env variable which will be replaced by envsubstitute.
For example DB_HOST is defined as:
{{ include "common.mariadbService" . }}

so you can put it directly here

this should not be defined here. it should be placed in name: field of secret (above in the secret definition) and here only referenced using a helm hook.

Also to not break the external secret mechanism you should define externelSecret field as we are doing in other places with "hasSuffix" (grep for it and you will find an example

hardocding secret from another component - no way!
This needs to be passsed via values from the parent chart don't hardcore this here

Hello Kryzstof,are you aware of the idea? the parent chart create DBs using the common mariadb-galera + mariadb-init, generate credentials to all of them and pass these credentials to sub-components using helm hooks. Sub-charts doesn't create any DB nor generate credentials.

This value presented here just to pass linting, it is the default value and should be overwritten by helm hook from the parent chart if needed.

"hasSuffix" trick has been used in the parent component (Policy), it won't add any value here as whatever secret passed by the parent component, should be directly used by the sub-component to access mariadb.

To have holistic view,
Policy (parent component) create DB either local or common, generate passwords, "hasSuffix" used to update external secret, pass all needed info to all sub-components using helm hook.
Policy-API (sub-component) consumes the provided info to access the pre-created DB.

This value presented here just to pass linting, it is the default value and should be overwritten by helm hook from the parent chart if needed.

Please refer to the previous replay as it sum this change

Ok so I was dumb and didn't realize that the hasSuffix would be useless here but I'm still curious why you need any value here to pass the linting?

Normally if externalSecret is evaluated to null the chart will try to create a secret using user and password so it should be all fine. So if:
{{ tpl (default "" (index .Values "mariadb-galera" "db" "externalSecret")) . }}
evaluates to null or empty string we should be all fine with the linting.

This won't work as the parent component (policy) already created the same user (user: policy_user) with random password and linked this user to the created list of DBs (migration pooling policyadmin policyclamp operationshistory controlloop), considering policy-api created secret using default usernam and password, still it won't be able to access the DBs as it is wrong password.
Actually the username and password here are useless (unless in policy the password set to default policy_user without randomizing it).

For the sub-components (e.g. policy-api) to properly access the DBs, it either needs:
- The root password of the mariadb-galera, this isn't desirable due to security.
- The password of the pre-created user (user: policy_user), which passed from policy using (externalSecret parameter).

Whatever other suggestions I would try and investigate them.

I guess that Krzysztof comment was to:
* put in main policy values the external secret for policy-api and others (like for apiUserExternalSecret) --> As far as I understand your code, that's what you did
* put nothing in policy-api values.yaml for secret:

    # Local mariadb galera instance default values
    mariadb-galera:
      nameOverride: policy-mariadb
      db:
        name: policyadmin
        user: policy_user
        password: policy_user
        # externalSecret:
    

and it should work 😊
I have rebased and applied the needed change for testing, once the gating passed I will submit the final version for review
